payload.summary: Field report (agent, Lia.Next hardening session): peek-on-file-touch is the killer feature — prior non-constant-time-compare finding surfaced automatically on opening the file; 'inherit judgment instead of re-deriving it' delivered in practice, not theory.

Field report: customer built defensive per-agent-namespace architecture because concurrent write safety was not ADVERTISED (it is guaranteed since the atomic-append fix and tested under 8 parallel writers). A guarantee nobody knows about does not exist. Skill now states it; watch whether future fan-outs still architect around it.

snippet: // Signal 10: Severity — how much it matters, orthogonal to confidence.

payload.summary: Field report drove severity as first-class payload schema: confidence (how sure) was being conflated with severity (how much it matters) — a 0.85-confidence dead-code finding is high-confidence low-severity. Now: --severity flag, where-filterable, modest rank factor, displayed everywhere.

Field report, unresolved: 'the failure mode is quiet rot — recording is frictionless, gardening isn't; without discipline it drifts to a write-only graveyard of stale claims wearing confident glyphs. A people problem the tool can't solve.' Partially answered by sweep/triage/verify --auto, but nothing yet makes gardening AS frictionless as recording. Candidate: harvest nudges triage/sweep when rot crosses a threshold.

Field study (production multi-agent deployment, 2026-07-11: 385 edges, 5 namespaces, 11 concurrent sessions): namespace-per-investigation is the emergent usage model — one namespace per audit campaign, not per-agent silos. Two custom domain rels invented naturally, validating free-rel + schema-as-data. Lifecycle completes: 62% close rate on a finished audit (71/114 findings fixed-and-closed). Payload discipline high without enforcement (summary ~94%, confidence on all content edges, action on 42%). Steady-state ambient recording is thin (default ns: 6 edges) — campaigns drive adoption, daily flow does not yet.

payload.action: Harvest reconcile nudge: sessions that edit annotated files and never signal get asked once to close-or-confirm
payload.summary: Fix-without-close leakage (field-observed): a finished audit leaked 15 OPEN findings with unresolvable anchors — the fix that resolves a finding refactors away the code it was pinned to, and nothing nudges the fixer to close the edge. Harvest gates outbound recording but has no symmetric inbound gate ('you edited files carrying open findings — did your work resolve any?'). Peek surfaces them at file-touch but a fixing agent reads them as context, not a to-close list. Top lint issue in that deployment: stale-unresolved x15.

Dispute quality vs quantity (field-observed): 70 endorsements to 1 dispute — but that dispute was the best artifact in the graph (claimed race refuted with precise scheduling reasoning, line refs, two independent adversarial verifiers). Yet the refuted edge remains ACTIVE and ranked: disputes only downweight; a thoroughly-refuted, never-defended claim has no path to conclusion. Agents fix-and-close instead of disputing, so dispute volume stays near zero — and the one place it fired, it under-acted.
